question: How are the four token-identification wells illuminated: one discrete RGB LED per bay with shared PWM channels, or one addressable (WS2812B-class) emitter per bay on a single data line?
context: The task allows 'one RGB illumination source per bay or a verified shared-source architecture'. The ESP32-C3 pin budget (DEC-001) leaves few free pins after 4 ADC inputs, audio, backlight, daughterboard I2C/INT, and the RGB function.

alternatives.description: Four WS2812B-class addressable RGB emitters, one physically inside each bay, all on one data line (GPIO10). During classification exactly one bay is illuminated (single-bay strobe policy); the other three are commanded dark.
alternatives.tradeoffs: Pros: 1 GPIO total; per-bay physical isolation of light sources; deterministic color commands; trivially extensible. Cons: ~1 mA quiescent per emitter when dark (budgeted in power-tree.json); 5 V-class part driven at 3.3 V data/VBAT supply needs level margin verified on bench (R-12 adjacent); color spectrum fixed by the part, not tunable analog.

alternatives.description: Common-anode discrete RGB LED per bay; three shared PWM channels plus four bay-enable transistors (7 GPIO total).
alternatives.tradeoffs: Pros: analog brightness control per channel, no digital-protocol timing concerns during ADC sampling. Cons: 7 GPIO does not fit the frozen pin map alongside the daughterboard interface; more BOM lines (4 transistors + 12 resistors).

rationale: The addressable chain is the only architecture that fits the frozen ESP32-C3 pin budget while keeping one physical emitter per bay. The 'shared source' is the data line, not the light: each bay has its own emitter and the single-bay strobe policy guarantees optical isolation during classification. Strobe-vs-ADC noise interaction is a registered risk (R-12) with a bench resolution path, not an assumed-safe condition.
tradeoffs: Accepts ~4 mA total quiescent draw and a bench obligation to verify data-line timing and ADC noise during strobes.

metadata.strobePolicy: single bay illuminated during classification; settling 5 ms (estimated)
